post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 185: Ancestor Mafia
Credits to Binomial-theorem/chaotic_iak/willwang123 (among others) for the rule template.

Story
In the Village, everyone was happy. People lived as farmers, worshipped their ancestors, and when they died, became ghosts. These ghosts helped the people by giving them advice, and everyone prospered. Until one day, some of the villagers got angry at the ancestors. The town woke up with all the ghosts nowhere to be found.
Zdtsd has been killed. He was Ancestral Protector (Moderator)
Kagebaka has been killed. He was Ancestral Spirit (Comoderator)
Then Kagebaka randomly became MY-2, who is the new comod.
Everyone was worried, and met in the town square to discuss what happened.


Rules
  • To confirm you are playing this game, post "Confirm".
  • Read the rules. Including this one.
  • Follow all the rules. Including this one.
  • Do not post at night.
  • You may PM me at any time to ask for rules clarification.
  • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs. There are no loopholes so don’t look for them.
  • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the moderator or co-moderator, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
  • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
  • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
  • Do not pretend to edit or otherwise pretend to break a rule.
  • If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere, other than the dead quicktopic. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread, unless directed otherwise by the moderator.


Voting/Hangs
  • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". For example, "Vote: zdtsd" is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: zdtsd", "Vote zdtsd", "vote: zdtsd", "Vote:zdtsd", "Vote: ztdtd", "Vote: zdtds", "Vote: scrub", "Vote: Zdtsd" and "Vote: zdtsd" are not.
  • Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
  • Improperly made votes (including votes in hide tags or any of the unacceptable forms above) will not be counted under any circumstances. I will not tell you if your vote is invalid. There is one exception to this; see below rule.
  • The characters "I" (capital i) and "l" (lowercase L) are considered the same character for the purpose of voting. That is, attempting to fakevote by substituting one of these characters in will not work. Most other fakevotes are acceptable, but please PM me to confirm that it is.
  • You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang".
  • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
  • This game uses a deadline hang, where the person with the highest number of votes at the deadline is hanged. If it is a tie, the hang is a random player with the most votes.
  • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
  • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
  • Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.
  • Some votecounts may or may not contain relevant, non-vote-based information.


Activity
  • Each day will be 336 hours (2 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
  • The deadline will occur the very second 336 hours has occurred.
  • If the deadline is met and no person is hanged, it will be considered no hang and will proceed to be twilight.
  • Each night will be 4 real-life days long.
  • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
  • Any night results will be sent after the night deadline.
  • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night and start of the next day.
  • If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive an in-game prod.
  • If a player does not post in another 36-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
  • If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless he/she posts before the moderator finds a replacement.
  • If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long. If your V/LA will be longer than about a week, please either try to find a way to participate in the game during that time or ask for a replacement.


Etiquette
  • Do not use inappropriate language.
  • Do not make any personal attacks.
  • Be mature.
  • For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to two warnings, SE’s up to one, and IC’s none. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled. Please do not make me modkill you.


Moderators
  • I (zdtsd) am the Moderator of Game 185.
  • MY-2 is the comoderator of Game 185.
  • Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator's posts. It will also be used for third party flavor-related posts.
  • Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
  • Purple-pink-ish-colored text (#A64D79) is used for general game flavor.
  • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.
  • As the moderator, I will not lie in the OP. I may or may not lie over the course of the game. I won't lie in Role PMs.


Setup
This game has a closed setup. It is BM, in that there are mechanics that are hidden from some or all players.

Daychat is not standard in Mafia games.

Both roleclaiming and flavorclaiming are allowed. The VT Role PM is the only role PM given, and is as follows:
Vanilla Townie Role PM wrote:
You are a Vanilla Townie. You are town aligned, obviously.

Thundius owl BZ1

kevinmathz Death Flip
AhirGauss Death
Kbwuf Death Flip

benjaminsz Death
The entirety of town Death and Flip


Warnings
GeneralCobra19: 1
Archimedes15: 2
thepiecingarrow: 1


Do not post until this line is crossed out!
Congratulations to chess24, hdgehrke, and Made_in_2004 on the Mafia victory!

Charge ISO v1
This is Charge's first meaningful post. The main issue with this is that it involves an obsession about jester as a role, adding on sentences to defend it, including a "it's RVS" defence. I guess this could be third party speculation, but most of the time it can be done without the extra jester fluff.
This is a minor post, with a good point recorded for the town of the sudden change in the VT role PM. The OMGUS is somewhat inscrutable, but NAI.
This is, along with the post two above, mostly a basic explanation. This also explains the negative effects of trying too hard to push the miller claim, which is mildly towny considering other positions from others.
This is basically a complaint about a mild feeling, which is lazy (he could ask why), but more lazy-indicative than mafia-indicative.
This shows a somewhat wrong (in my opinion) idea of mafia targetting. Generally, confirmed town narrows the pool, acting as a correct and inarguable town read, unlike other, better, town reads which can be changed. It is a general good game idea to make sure to check up on Archimedes15's reads, but it doesn't really have a goal suggested.
This is a good focus on Made_in_2004's lack of contribution, and another defense of NDMath.
This is one of the first really confusing posts made by Charge. He says that agreeing is bad, as compared to agreeing without reason, that NDMath is anti-town for saying anti-town exists, and that gut feelings can't be explained.
This is a walkback, saying that NDMath is likely town, unless he is a third party cult member. This mostly cancels out the prior post, though there is a continuing fixation on third parties.
This is also weird, since it defends SFGF's weird doublevoter claim, stating that a mafia doublevoter is unlikely. Even if there was mod cover, Charge still doesn't find it suspicious, which doesn't make sense.
This and this are both continuations of a defense of the miller claim as NAI, which is correct.
This is also reasonable, being a viewpoint I agree with.
This is a post encouraging others to get on track and focus on the actual game.
This is another simple stop-bandwagoning post and a go-contribute post.
This is a weak jester justification via “I love it”, which is a rare opinion which doesn’t explain the obsession with it anyways.
This is mostly about stating that a claim would be impossible, despite earlier efforts at attempting to stop claiming (which was correct).
This tries to resolve the jester claim, without resolving the idea of a gambit as mafia to try to subtly claim jester.
This is generic good play advice.
This is a blatant plea at ignorance and an attempt to distract from jester claims.
This is a dodge at defending and a shift to targeting GeneralCobra19, despite acknowledging his reasoning was correct.
This post has a simple answer. We focused on the claims to get out of random voting stage, and because it is an important mechanical decision of dubious importance, it invites attention.
This is a null post asking about a previous post.
This post appears to give a genuine reaction to being accused of meta abuse, which does not appear to be happening.
This post is another accusation at non-contributors, which feels NAI enough.
This post is a good callout for some dumb self-victimization.
This post is NAI. This counterargument would be the basic one done by both town and mafia.
This post is weird since it seems to try to avoid a w/w interaction by saying a “I need to do an investigation” is sufficient.
This post seems like bad theory, since a miller is anti-town because it hinders a cop from getting conftown/confmafia and allows mafia cover, unlike a vanilla townie.
This post makes logical sense, but doesn’t have much other relevant content.
This post makes a point about GeneralCobra19 trying to hide behind “jokes”, but it ignores the point about it being a bad idea to try to attack NDMath’s miller claim.
This post ignores the miller point Made_in_2004 was trying to make, like the prior post.
This post and This post both deal with critiques of GeneralCobra19’s ISO. It poses reasonable concerns, especially about the unmarked missing posts (which should be labeled as fluff).
This post is mostly null, since it is a giving of credit and a denial of a statement, instead of looking at the implication of the statement which is also pretty benign.
This post encouraged me to analyze him, to show my lack of fake contribution.
This post attempts to get a false conclusion from one post. Mentioning a role is not equivalent to having the role, like how mentioning jester is not equivalent to being jester.
This post is basically a simple game question.
This post simultaneously says a godfather is unlikely while entertaining 15 millers. However, it does support stopping talking about claims, so that is a net positive.
This post is mostly cancelled out by his next post here. The questions are, however, mostly good.
This post is a post honest about the uncertainty, since it talks in 0.00001%. This feels towny.
This post feels off. Vote counts in BM games are important (see the holmes mafia) and it requires switching resources from scum hunting. Also, agreeing is likely to be consensus, unless all posters are mafia.
This post appears to be mainly another accusation of non-contribution. (FINISH)

Unanalyzed posts
This since it is the confirm post.
This since it doesn't really have anything of significant content.
This for similar reasons.
This for similar reasons.
This for similar reasons.

I do agree this logic is faulty if used on this own. But as I said, I've actually explained my scumreads in an analysis earlier and that's before I thought of this theory. These scumreads match my WIFOM logic. WIFOM is a crucial part of mafia (you can also see in my games that I was an innovator in the forum that way where I was really the first to use WIFOM as actual good evidence, and it works if you have other things to back it up). If you don't have any WIFOM (facts about people who died, generally how mafia plays, how town plays etc) to backup your scumreads, your scumreads are a lot of the time wrong. It's the same thing vice versa. If you can't find anything scummy about how a player posts, but have WIFOM that makes you think they should have been killed, or something should have happened differently if they were town, they are also probably town. On chess, charge, and sudz, I've got both. Look at charge today compared to previous days: huge contrast in playstyle, from very townie to very scummy. Also I have read that apparently charge is not very good at scum and gives off a lot of scummy vibes when scum which he definitely has in D5. chess has the WIFOM where he was against the GC hang and somehow survived N2 despite carrying hard D2. I've also included how scummy he looks in D3ish onwards in my reads with actually some more WIFOM there that supports this. sudz led a hang on GC (so WIFOM shows he was not cult at this point, could be mafia but not cult). However, D3 and on he is hella scummy. After N2 he did not support a single accusation with any facts at all. Therefore, very scummy reads with WIFOM that support my theory (but doesn't necessarily support him being scum 100% which is why it is important to consider reads and WIFOM at the same time).

Anyway, hope you understand what I'm saying. I think I'm the only one on this forum to adopt this strategy before and after my time, but it has evidence that it works, and that is why I've crafted this theory because it supports all the evidence that I gathered reading through the game. As Sherlock Holmes says, "After you've eliminated the impossible, whatever remains, no matter however improbably, must be the truth." Which is exactly what I've done here. The probable: there is town, mafia, and cult. That is basically impossible based on what I've analyzed. Next probable: there is town, mafia, and cult has died. This is also pretty impossible based on how players act one day compared to another plus the cop claim (benj has inspected 4 of 9 players alive, got inno on 3, no result on 1, if mafia there should have been a 95% chance or so he would have inspected one unless 4 of hdge, made, BZ1, wizard, chess, and me are mafia. WIFOM yes, but it also supports my reads that this isn't the case). Finally, what is left? There only were ever two factions: town and cult. Now, besides some details, this theory actually fits very well.

If you have another theory, please let me know. I feel like I have exhausted all of them and have come to this conclusion.

Actually, because of the dead forum, I think the game is more town sided than mafia sided. The dead forum makes the game more balanced because if the Mafia are stronger than Town, the town won't lose power as quickly. Also, sudz was scum, he was a cultist.

I don't know how to share the dead forum, but here is the list of roles and what the ones I made up do.
Roles wrote:
The Cult Compulsive Even Night Sanctifier Odd Night Leader(Cult High Priest) is an odd night cult recruiter that must target a dead player each even night. They are told it reveals their role to everyone, but it also removes their ghost from the game entirely(they are not told this). Cannot convert Mafia, and converts lose all abilities. Converting Innocent Child kills it instead.

The Vanilla and Evil Ghostie roles have no special abilities.

The Jobber Ghostie is a jobber with much weaker abilities, which are making a person Hated the next day, Loved the next day, tracking a person, and sending a 25 character message to a person.

The Smart Ghostie has a 1-shot Cop ability.

Loud Ghostie is a doublevoter in the ghost vote, and can send me a message of up to 15 characters to post in the normal thread that night. It cannot refer to the dead forum, which is mostly at moderator's discretion(no "PplRGhostsNDeth", but "CopSayStatsScum" is OK. This is the only communication with this restriction, due to telling everyone).

Polterghostie is a 1-shot mass ghost roleblocker that doesn't affect the Mafia actions. Using this ability causes the player to be removed from the game as though targeted by the Cult Sanctifier. The polterghostie can also still post in the mafia quicktopic, which lets the Mafia discover this mechanic.

Deathghast is a doublevoter that can kill any one living player at any time. Using this ability causes the player to be removed from the game as though targeted by the Cult Sanctifier.

Ghostie in Denial keeps the sanctification ability and is told exactly how it works upon death.

Pious Ghostie doesn't get access to the ghost forum, or knowledge of it. Instead, the player is told that they can post in the main thread, but only at night. It can still be targeted by the Cult Sanctifier, and it cannot vote.

Sanctity Spirit keeps its Odd Night Sanctify ability.

When a cultist dies, they don't become a Ghost, they are removed from the game entirely and their role revealed.

I don't know how to share the dead forum, but here is the list of roles and what the ones I made up do.

[quote=Roles]The Cult Compulsive Even Night Sanctifier Odd Night Leader(Cult High Priest) is an odd night cult recruiter that must target a dead player each even night. They are told it reveals their role to everyone, but it also removes their ghost from the game entirely(they are not told this). Cannot convert Mafia, and converts lose all abilities. Converting Innocent Child kills it instead.

The Vanilla and Evil Ghostie roles have no special abilities.

The Jobber Ghostie is a jobber with much weaker abilities, which are making a person Hated the next day, Loved the next day, tracking a person, and sending a 25 character message to a person.

The Smart Ghostie has a 1-shot Cop ability.

Loud Ghostie is a doublevoter in the ghost vote, and can send me a message of up to 15 characters to post in the normal thread that night. It cannot refer to the dead forum, which is mostly at moderator's discretion(no "PplRGhostsNDeth", but "CopSayStatsScum" is OK. This is the only communication with this restriction, due to telling everyone).

Polterghostie is a 1-shot mass ghost roleblocker that doesn't affect the Mafia actions. Using this ability causes the player to be removed from the game as though targeted by the Cult Sanctifier. The polterghostie can also still post in the mafia quicktopic, which lets the Mafia discover this mechanic.

Deathghast is a doublevoter that can kill any one living player at any time. Using this ability causes the player to be removed from the game as though targeted by the Cult Sanctifier.

Ghostie in Denial keeps the sanctification ability and is told exactly how it works upon death.

Pious Ghostie doesn't get access to the ghost forum, or knowledge of it. Instead, the player is told that they can post in the main thread, but only at night. It can still be targeted by the Cult Sanctifier, and it cannot vote.

Sanctity Spirit keeps its Odd Night Sanctify ability.

When a cultist dies, they don't become a Ghost, they are removed from the game entirely and their role revealed.[/quote]
